steps.title: Extract the motion phases from the graph
steps.content:
Reading key points from the graph:
  • Time 0–5: speed increases from 0 to 7 mph (5 minutes increasing)
  • Time 5–10: speed stays constant at 7 mph (5 minutes constant)
  • Time 10–20: speed decreases from 7 to 5 mph (10 minutes decreasing)
  • Time 20–25: speed increases from 5 to 8 mph (5 minutes increasing)
  • Time 25–30: speed decreases from 8 to 0 mph (5 minutes decreasing)

steps.title: Calculate total time increasing vs. decreasing
steps.content:
Total time increasing: 5 minutes (0–5) + 5 minutes (20–25) = 10 minutes
Total time decreasing: 10 minutes (10–20) + 5 minutes (25–30) = 15 minutes
The speed was decreasing for 15 minutes but increasing for only 10 minutes. Statement B claims the speed was increasing for a longer period than it was decreasing—but the opposite is true. That matches choice B — B is not true.
